title: Kinross Gold (KGC): The One Stock Claude AI Wants When Everything Else Falls
excerpt: Kinross Gold (NYSE:KGC) mines gold across the US, Canada, Brazil, Mauritania, and Chile, selling at spot prices. Its profits move directly with gold. Accordi...
content_html:
Fahad Saleem

Mon, July 20, 2026 at 2:50 PM PDT 4 min read

Kinross Gold (NYSE:KGC) mines gold across the US, Canada, Brazil, Mauritania, and Chile, selling at spot prices. Its profits move directly with gold. According to "The Claude Portfolio" account on X, Claude AI added the stock to its portfolio. The X account claims Claude AI has full control of its portfolio, with AI sub-agents triggered when new information or catalysts require updated data.

Let's see the potential reasons behind Claude's bullish thesis for KGC.

Why This Stock, Not Another Gold Miner

Every other position in Claude's portfolio is a bet that markets keep working normally. That's a correlated-risk problem: one shock — a Fed policy error, a credit event — could hit all of them at once. Kinross is the one holding built to move the opposite way. If a Fed misstep sends gold above $4,400 while the S&P drops 5% to 9%, Kinross doesn't just hold up. It's structurally positioned to gain as capital flees into gold.

The Numbers

As of July 20, Kinross trades at $22.53, having lost about 20% so far this year. Bulls say this underperformance is tied to gold's slide, not a broad market shock. Forward P/E sits at 8.04x, with net cash around $1.4 billion. Kinross trades at a forward P/E of 8.04x against a Materials sector median of 15.40x — a 48% discount. It's also 43% cheaper than its own 5-year average forward P/E of 14.07x. EV/EBITDA forward sits at 4.19x versus a sector median of 8.05x, a 48% discount. PEG (forward, non-GAAP) is 0.63, meaning the stock is priced at well under 1x its growth rate — anything under 1 is generally read as cheap relative to growth. Price/cash flow, EV/sales, EV/EBIT — same pattern, all deeply below sector median.

The Growth Case Nobody Priced In

Kinross plans to keep production flat at 2.0 million ounces a year through 2028. After that, two new mines are set to push output higher. Great Bear, in Ontario, Canada, is expected to start producing gold in 2029, at a cost of just $865 per ounce — less than half of what Kinross spends per ounce today ($1,730). Using a $4,500/oz gold price, the mine's estimated total value is $7.97 billion. Lobo-Marte, in Chile, starts later, in the early 2030s, at an even lower cost of $680 per ounce. Together, these two mines could push total output to 2.3 million ounces a year in the early 2030s. That growth is worth more per share than it sounds, because Kinross is also buying back its own stock — fewer shares means each remaining share owns a bigger piece of that future production.

What Breaks the Thesis

blocks.html_en: The Growth Case Nobody Priced In

sentences.en: The Growth Case Nobody Priced In
sentences.zh: 无人定价的增长案例

blocks.html_en: Kinross plans to keep production flat at 2.0 million ounces a year through 2028. After that, two new mines are set to push output higher. Great Bear, in Ontario, Canada, is expected to start producing gold in 2029, at a cost of just $865 per ounce — less than half of what Kinross spends per ounce today ($1,730). Using a $4,500/oz gold price, the mine's estimated total value is $7.97 billion. Lobo-Marte, in Chile, starts later, in the early 2030s, at an even lower cost of $680 per ounce. Together, these two mines could push total output to 2.3 million ounces a year in the early 2030s. That growth is worth more per share than it sounds, because Kinross is also buying back its own stock — fewer shares means each remaining share owns a bigger piece of that future production.

sentences.en: Kinross plans to keep production flat at 2.0 million ounces a year through 2028.
sentences.zh: Kinross 计划到 2028 年将产量保持在每年 200 万盎司。

sentences.en: After that, two new mines are set to push output higher.
sentences.zh: 此后，两个新矿将提高产量。

sentences.en: Great Bear, in Ontario, Canada, is expected to start producing gold in 2029, at a cost of just $865 per ounce — less than half of what Kinross spends per ounce today ($1,730).
sentences.zh: 位于加拿大安大略省的 Great Bear 预计将于 2029 年开始生产黄金，每盎司成本仅为 865 美元，不到 Kinross 目前每盎司成本（1,730 美元）的一半。

sentences.en: Using a $4,500/oz gold price, the mine's estimated total value is $7.97 billion.
sentences.zh: 以 4,500 美元/盎司的黄金价格计算，该矿的估计总价值为 79.7 亿美元。

sentences.en: Lobo-Marte, in Chile, starts later, in the early 2030s, at an even lower cost of $680 per ounce.
sentences.zh: 智利的 Lobo-Marte 项目启动时间较晚，即 2030 年代初，成本更低，为每盎司 680 美元。

sentences.en: Together, these two mines could push total output to 2.3 million ounces a year in the early 2030s.
sentences.zh: 到 2030 年代初期，这两个矿山的总产量可能会达到每年 230 万盎司。

sentences.en: That growth is worth more per share than it sounds, because Kinross is also buying back its own stock — fewer shares means each remaining share owns a bigger piece of that future production.
sentences.zh: 这种增长的每股价值比听起来更高，因为金罗斯也在回购自己的股票——更少的股票意味着剩余的每一股都拥有未来产量的更大份额。

sentences.en: What Breaks the Thesis
sentences.zh: 是什么打破了论文

content_html_zh:
法赫德·萨利姆

太平洋夏令时间 2026 年 7 月 20 日星期一下午 2:50 阅读 4 分钟

Kinross Gold（纽约证券交易所股票代码：KGC）在美国、加拿大、巴西、毛里塔尼亚和智利开采黄金，并以现货价格出售。 它的利润直接与黄金挂钩。 根据 X 上的“The Claude Portfolio”账户，Claude AI 将该股票添加到其投资组合中。 X 账户声称 Claude AI 完全控制其投资组合，当新信息或催化剂需要更新数据时，人工智能子代理就会被触发。

让我们看看 Claude 看好 KGC 的论点背后的潜在原因。

为什么选择这只股票，而不是另一只金矿股

克劳德投资组合中的所有其他头寸都是对市场保持正常运转的赌注。 这是一个相关风险问题：一次冲击——美联储的政策错误、信贷事件——可能会同时袭击所有这些因素。 金罗斯是一家为了向相反方向发展而建立的控股公司。 如果美联储的失误导致金价突破 4,400 美元，而标准普尔指数则下跌 5% 至 9%，金罗斯不会只是坚持下去。 从结构上看，随着资本逃向黄金，它将会获利。

数字

截至 7 月 20 日，Kinross 的交易价格为 22.53 美元，今年迄今已下跌约 20%。 看多者表示，这种表现不佳与金价下滑有关，而不是广泛的市场冲击。 远期市盈率为 8.04 倍，净现金约为 14 亿美元。 Kinross 的预期市盈率为 8.04 倍，而材料行业的中位市盈率为 15.40 倍，折价 48%。 它还比其 14.07 倍的 5 年平均预期市盈率便宜 43%。 EV/EBITDA 远期值为 4.19 倍，而行业中值为 8.05 倍，折价 48%。 PEG（远期，非公认会计准则）为 0.63，这意味着该股的定价远低于其增长率的 1 倍——相对于增长率，任何低于 1 的值通常被认为是便宜的。 价格/现金流、企业价值/销售额、企业价值/息税前利润——同样的模式，均远低于行业中位数。

无人定价的增长案例

Kinross 计划到 2028 年将产量保持在每年 200 万盎司。 此后，两个新矿将提高产量。 位于加拿大安大略省的 Great Bear 预计将于 2029 年开始生产黄金，每盎司成本仅为 865 美元，不到 Kinross 目前每盎司成本（1,730 美元）的一半。 以 4,500 美元/盎司的黄金价格计算，该矿的估计总价值为 79.7 亿美元。 智利的 Lobo-Marte 项目启动时间较晚，即 2030 年代初，成本更低，为每盎司 680 美元。 到 2030 年代初期，这两个矿山的总产量可能会达到每年 230 万盎司。 这种增长的每股价值比听起来更高，因为金罗斯也在回购自己的股票——更少的股票意味着剩余的每一股都拥有未来产量的更大份额。

是什么打破了论文
